title: Viz Project Document (schema-v1)
description: The single versioned JSON project document for the visualization platform (ADR-D4 serialization-first; INV-5). EVERY scene/object/dynamic/effect IS its declarative spec - the runtime handle is derived from the spec, never the reverse. This one document is the load/save surface, the AI-mutation target, and the conformance-vector input. NO logic is serialized as functions or 'eval' (declarative parameter maps ONLY). Objects embed the D3 EncodeSpec field; effects embed three.quarks ParticleSystem JSON. saveProject() serializes registry ids + params; loadProject() re-creates handles by calling factories with the saved params - never live GPU objects. This schema is engine-only: it names no app-specific (consumer) concepts.

format.description: Fixed magic string identifying a viz project document. MUST be exactly this constant so a loader can reject foreign JSON before migration.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of the document shape (ADR-D4). Forward-migrating loaders in engine/serialize key on this; a bump is a breaking change. v1 of this skeleton is 1.0.0.

data.description: Declarative data-source descriptors (NDArray metadata + a source reference). NEVER the live typed-array buffer - only id + kind + shape + a src reference, so the document round-trips without embedding GPU/binary state.

scenes.description: Scene descriptors (Pillar 1). Each binds a SceneRegistry id + params and may reference objects/dynamics/effects by id, plus declarative camera/light/transition param maps.

objects.description: Object descriptors (Pillar 2). Each binds an ObjectRegistry id + params and embeds the D3 EncodeSpec in its 'encode' field.

dynamics.description: Dynamics descriptors (Pillar 3). Each binds a DynamicsRegistry id + declarative params (timeline/keyframe/trajectory parameter maps only - no serialized functions).

effects.description: Effect descriptors (Pillar 4 / VFX). Each binds a VfxRegistry id + params and MAY embed three.quarks ParticleSystem JSON in its 'quarks' field.

id.description: A stable, content-stable identifier. No floats-as-ids, no random ids (deterministic + content-derived where stable identity matters).

registryId.description: A registry key (the capability id a registry's register()/list() exposes). Renaming a registry id is a breaking, versioned change (the conformance suite keys on these strings).

dataKind.description: The frozen data-kind vocabulary (Consistency Rules). Drives default projection and which dynamics apply.

channel.description: The frozen, closed channel set (Consistency Rules). The only channels an EncodeSpec may target.

params.description: A declarative parameter map - plain JSON scalars/arrays/nested objects only. NO functions, NO 'eval', NO serialized code (INV-5). This is the only way params are carried for any registry capability.

shape.description: NDArray shape (e.g. [rows, cols] or [t, n, f]). Metadata only - never the backing buffer.

src.description: A declarative reference to where the data is loaded from (a URL/path/handle key) - NEVER inline binary GPU/typed-array state.

encodeBinding.description: One channel binding in an EncodeSpec: a data accessor resolved through one explicit scale. Fully declarative - 'from' is an accessor string (e.g. '@data.netWorth' / '@proj'), never a function.

from.description: Data accessor string ('@data.<col>' or '@<projectionAlias>'). Declarative reference only.

channel.description: Optional sub-channel name for effectParam bindings (e.g. 'emissiveIntensity').

value.description: An optional constant literal value (used when a channel is a fixed datum rather than data-bound).

axis.description: Optional axis hint (e.g. 'y' for a scale channel).

map.description: Colormap name (when type === 'colormap'); resolves to a colour-blind-safe Lut. Output rides the sub-white emissive clamp (INV-3).

encodeSpec.description: The D3 EncodeSpec embedded in an object: a declarative {data, transform[], object, encode{channel->binding}} grammar. encode keys are drawn ONLY from the closed channel set. Pure + serializable (the conformance-vector boundary).

data.description: Reference to the data source feeding this encode (by id) plus optional inline kind/shape hints.

transform.description: Ordered list of declarative transform steps (projections/reducers); each names an op + params, never a function.

encode.description: channel -> binding map. Keys MUST be members of the closed channel set.

objects.description: Object ids bound into this scene (references into the top-level objects[] or inline object descriptors).

dynamics.description: Dynamics bound to this object (inline dynamic descriptors).

effects.description: Effects bound to this object (inline effect descriptors).

quarks.description: Optional embedded three.quarks ParticleSystem JSON (the proven VFX serialization bridge). Plain JSON parameter data only - no functions.
